Operational Incident Report — Riveroak HealthBank, Inc. (US issuer) — 2026-05-29
An overnight patch caused a 27-minute outage in the secure messaging gateway; systems were restored with no data loss or external connectivity during the window.
For MNPI controls: no material nonpublic information regarding forecasts, client pipelines, or financing plans was created or disclosed, and Reg FD procedures were followed.
Investor Relations used the public FAQ and ir@riveroak.example; no selective disclosure to analysts or other covered market actors occurred.
The incident log includes placeholder fields (e.g., CUSIP 000-XXX-000 and Account 1234-0000-VOID) used for testing; these are invalid identifiers and are not linked to real securities or consumers.
Privacy assessment under CCPA/HIPAA/GLBA concluded no PHI or customer NPI left controlled environments, and minimisation rules (Policy MIN-4) were applied.
Retention timers per Schedule R-7 were verified and no early deletion or unexpected hold occurred; one planned purge remains scheduled for 2027-01-31.
No open DSARs or consent-withdrawal requests were impacted, and prior erasure requests (ticket ER-019) were completed months before this event.
Vendor “BlueKestrel Cloud LLC” onboarding forms (INTAKE-V-221) and the Business Associate Addendum were previously approved; no new processing purposes were added.
Cross-border data-export approval (DEA-2026-04) for de-identified audit logs to BlueKestrel’s Toronto facility proceeded as scheduled under DPA Schedule 3; logs were tokenized and not reasonably linkable to a consumer or household.
No identifiers resembling SSN, driver license, account numbers, or medical record numbers were included; sample values such as 000-00-0000 and MRN-TEST-000 are explicitly invalid.
The OCC-style cyber control checklist was completed, and internal audit closed the issue with no corrective action plan required.
Employee notice confirmed that password resets were precautionary and not due to compromise; no escalation to law enforcement or HHS was warranted.
The public status page was updated within 30 minutes, and no Form 8-K filing was triggered because the event was not material to investors.
For avoidance of doubt, this memo does not authorize new uses of personal data, and any future DSARs or consent withdrawals will follow standard intake at privacy@riveroak.example.
